textPlain:
I wonder why round-trip is such a small concern for people implementing serializers/deserializers of various kinds.
I usually throw in an "Unknown" node type, which stores things unaltered until I can understand things again. The parsers I usually write are very small, so I haven't seen what issues comes up at scale, maybe there are dragons lurking .
would be cool if they published this as oss
This is the solution for that particular issue that Tritium uses.[NOTE: the dragons would be the memory consumption alluded to in the article.]

Thanks for the kind words, and I have given it serious thought.It's definitely not impossible in the future.I just don't think there is enough interest right now in contributing to the underlying tech without generalizing it so much that it basically becomes an inferior LibreOffice.Instead, the business model for Tritium is to give away the niche legal product for free to the community, but charge commercial users who need more granular control over its network activity, etc. This gives smaller start-ups, law offices and in-house shops a chance to benefit from the hopefully useful features while reserving for more demanding organizations to express an interest in and benefit from advanced features.
or - if feasible - extend the existing crate instead of creating a new one.
